_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to project.json inside the project's mount directory (typically /data/<collection>/<project>/project.json) and fill in. This .example file is safe to commit. The real project.json carries project-specific paths and stakeholder emails; edit per project. Comments are inline as '_comment_*' keys (JSON has no comment syntax); the pipeline ignores keys starting with '_comment'.
_comment_project_metadata: Descriptive fields shown to humans (PI, study description, sites, cohorts, visit names). The pipeline uses project_common_name in logs/emails; the rest is reference material for stakeholders and the LORIS module that surfaces this data.
_comment_clinical_instruments: List of instrument short-names expected in this project's clinical data. Used by LORIS-side validation and the data-dictionary pipeline; the clinical ingestion pipeline derives instruments from CSV headers and filenames at runtime, so this list is for stakeholders/LORIS, not for the pipeline.
_comment_data_access: Where the pipeline reads from. mount_enabled gates whether the project is processed at all; mount_path is the root for the project on the pipeline host. deidentified-raw/, documentation/, processed/, and logs/ sit under it.
_comment_candidate_defaults: Fallback values used when a candidate row in clinical data is missing one of these fields. Applied at ingestion time on a per-field basis. Keep dob conservative (e.g. 1970-01-01) — it should never be a real DOB.
_comment_project_mappings: Map from in-data project label to LORIS internal project ID. Used by LORIS API calls that need a numeric project ID; keys here should match the values that appear in candidate_defaults.project and in the clinical CSVs.
_comment_participant_metadata: How the pipeline locates participant-level metadata (e.g. Date of Death) and merges it into LORIS. Two source kinds: (a) the BIDS/clinical data files themselves (defaults.bids and defaults.clinical) — identifier_field is the column that holds the participant id, optional identifier_strip_prefix strips 'sub-' from BIDS-style ids; fields maps the LORIS metadata key to an array of acceptable column name aliases. (b) Separate registry CSV files listed under 'sources'. Both kinds can coexist; values from sources are merged on top of data-file values. Omit this whole block if the project has no participant metadata.
_comment_notification_emails: Per-modality recipient lists. Each modality has on_success (run completed cleanly) and on_error (any failure) channels — the pipeline picks the list based on outcome. The 'enabled' key under each modality is reserved for future use and is currently ignored by the code; presence of a non-empty list is what makes notifications go out.
_comment_notification_evidata: The 'evidata' block UNDER notification_emails is for PRIVACY-CHECK failure recipients (on_check_failed) — completely separate from the top-level 'evidata' block at the bottom of this file, which controls QI policy. They share a name on purpose: notifications about EviData go here, configuration for EviData goes there.
_comment_evidata: EviData QI policy for THIS project (per-project override of the global evidata_config.json -> qis). Resolution: if 'qis' here is non-empty -> used as-is; if empty/absent -> falls back to global; if global also empty -> ALL-HEADERS mode (every CSV column becomes a QI, minus exclude_qis, minus unique-per-row identifier columns).
_comment_evidata_qis_shapes: Two shapes are accepted: (a) flat array applied to every clinical CSV in the project (e.g. ['Candidate_Age','Sex']), or (b) map with '_default' + per-filename overrides (e.g. {'_default':['Sex'], 'redcap_data.csv':['dob','sex']}). EviData's server-side QI validation is CASE-SENSITIVE — spell every QI exactly as it appears in that file's CSV header. Set qis to [] (or omit) to deliberately use the all-headers default for this project.
_comment_evidata_exclude_qis: Optional. Column names to DROP in all-headers mode (ignored when an explicit qis list is in effect). CASE-INSENSITIVE matching. Use it to remove known non-identifying columns or identifier columns (study IDs, visit labels, comment fields) from the all-headers set, on top of the automatic unique-per-row removal.
_comment_logging: Per-project logging. log_path defaults to <mount_path>/logs when omitted; level is one of DEBUG / INFO / WARNING / ERROR.
